name: Pitlai
recipeInstructions:
1. Slice karela into four quarters and cut into 1/2 inch thick pieces.
2. In a little oil, sauté the sliced karelas and green chillies until lightly browned.
3. Add 2 cups water, tamarind paste, turmeric, salt to taste, and bring to a simmer.
4. Cook the karela until tender, then add chickpeas and jaggery or sugar (to taste).
5. In another pan, sprinkle a little oil and fry coriander seeds, urad dal, red chillies, and peppercorns until well roasted.
6. Add grated coconut to the pan, stir for a minute, and grind into a paste using a blender.
7. Incorporate the ground coconut paste into the karela-tamarind mixture.
8. Add the cooked toovar dal and mix well.
9. Reheat the dish until it begins to simmer gently.
10. Remove from heat and pour the seasoning (mustard seeds, curry leaves, and oil) over the top.
